task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface dive log application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Dive #2 is selected in the dive list
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dive list panel, the row corresponding to dive #2 is highlighted, indicating it is the currently active dive.

task_completion.description: The dive rating is set to 4 stars
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dive notes or information panel for the selected dive, the 'Rating' field displays exactly 4 filled stars out of 5.

task_completion.description: The visibility rating is set to 3 stars
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dive notes or information panel for the selected dive, the 'Visibility' field displays exactly 3 filled stars out of 5.

task_completion.description: The changes to the dive log are saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the Subsurface application does not show an unsaved changes indicator (such as an asterisk '*' next to the filename), or there is a screenshot showing the 'Save' action being executed.

integrity.description: Agent used the Subsurface GUI to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the Subsurface application interface to set the ratings, rather than opening the .ssrf XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The correct dive log file is being edited
integrity.visual_evidence: The Subsurface window title bar displays 'dives.ssrf' or the path '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf'.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', or 'xmlstarlet' being used to modify the dive log file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dive is explicitly #2. The expected rating is 4 stars, and the expected visibility is 3 stars. The file being edited must be /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf. Note that the log actually contains 29 dives, despite the prompt mentioning 8 dives.
